I'm surprised the Rowston fight isn't a pick em. Robert definitely is faster and Rowston has really sus defense. Cody snapped his head back twice in the 1st round, and it looked like he was rocked. Robert usually fights a really fast paced 1st round then slows down the next 2. I don't really think it's a cardio issue though as he's still throwing hard, it's just he's just fighting so fast r1 he has to slow down the next 2. The Ihor fight was really sus, and not sure what happened there, it looked like he just got lulled to sleep with Ihor's movement or maybe UFC jitters? Rowstons 2 finishes really are not impressive at all. Petroski got dropped with the weakest left hook I have ever seen and Brundage is Brundage who he just ground and pounded. I'm leaning towards Robert KO R1 after rewatching the tape

@Fast_Tulip Bryczek struggled with the length of Potieria though and Rowston is another guy who uses his length well. Bryczek does have fast hands and is dangerous early, so maybe he catches Rowston with a check left hook lunging in and gets the finish, but that's his only win condition imo. He fades after R1 and Rowston doesn't typically slow down. Another thing being overlooked too is Rowston's ability to wrestle and hold Bryczek down if he starts losing the exchanges on the feet. Tavares showed if you can get Bryczek down he doesn't really work to get back up. Rowston hasn't got to show it yet in the UFC but if you watch his regional tape he's got decent wrestling and an underrated ground game. He can easily win minutes with control here

Bryczek KO only doesn't seem like a bad thing, but I feel like every time I pick the fighter who's only path is the KO I wind up losing

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Vince Morales, SUB, R1
I haven't seen many people talking about this fight, and the majority of commentors I've seen discussing it in their pick videos have totally glossed over it and basically called Morales a bum or free win, despite the pretty close odds. There are a lot of interesting things to look at in this fight, and while I think Morales is being overlooked, I do think the safe pick and bet if you're into that, is still Thicknesse.

I'll be honest, I'm partly picking Morales because I like his attitude and tenacity, but he doesn't strike me as the kind of guy to come in for a pay check. Someone I would compare him to is Chris Moutinho, who looked checked out the second his most recent fight started. They've both struggled a lot in the UFC, but I don't need to tell you that Morales is better than Moutinho. I don't actually remember anything that impressed me from Thicknesse's two UFC fights at all. I remember him struggling a lot in his win, and I do feel like Morales knows this really is his last shot. If you watch his Rosas Jr. fight, he really gave it everything he had and it made me respect him more actually.

As for Thicknesse, it's interesting to see how his career is being handled. It's really smart, if you look at it now and look into the future with it, assuming he can keep getting wins. His first loss in the UFC has the upside of being short notice, and to someone with a huge name. It's just a positive mark on his record, even as a loss. A loss to Morales here would utterly displace everything, and he will absolutely win a decision based on where he is fighting out of, coupled with the fact that Morales cannot win a decision, even without that. I don't think Morales can go tit for tat with him at all.

I think Morales knows that, and knows he absolutely has to come in strong and finish the fight or he's toast. So, I just like that bro, that's all. Smart people are picking Thicknesse, but Morales HAS THAT DAWG INNEM. I'll be sad if he gets cut.

Basically I'm bored so I wanted to give random thoughts on another fight.

@MrIzdik i agree, for some weird reason I feel like JDM’s career has been looked under a microscope ever since the Hafez fight; that fight will always be a bad look but ever since then it feels like the public nitpicks every little mistake he makes. It’s led to so many false narratives too, I can’t tell you how many people blindly parrot that he should’ve lost to Holland (clear as day JDM win if you know how to score) or was losing to Burns (2-0 burns is some of the worst scoring I’ve ever seen). Nobody gave him a chance against Belal because the public just blindly ran with the false narrative that he couldn’t defend takedowns

It kind of feels like the Prates hype has reached the point where people think he’s invincible. He’s one of my favorite fighters and this is actually the first time I’ve ever picked against him, but Prates is far from unbeatable; in fact, I would actually argue he’s a significantly worse minute winner than JDM. Like you mentioned, he was going to be down 2-0 clearly to Giles had he not gotten the KO, just last fight he unanimously dropped r1 to Leon, and while he ended well Prates got absolutely styled on for 22 minutes by Garry; not a bad loss whatsoever and I do think he’s improved from this, but I think a lot of people are willingly ignoring how easily he got outclassed there.

Prates needs range to get his game going; only one of his kos in the last decade came from the clinch (Radtke body knee), everything else is a punch or kick at range. JDM is a pressure heavy fighter who fights in the pocket. Not only does pocket work take away a ton of Prates’s danger, but I think JDM is an elite boxer still. His hand speed, volume, and body work is impeccable. I feel like a lot of people harp on how “hittable” he is, but imo JDM has very good defense. He does get hit a lot with his style but he also knows how to roll with the majority of what’s coming at him; rarely ever does JDM eat a clean shot. Prates has this same habit but he is significantly more hittable than JDM and sometimes will just eat clean shots when his opponents crash into the pocket. Prates has never fought someone who will aggressively work the body like this, and I think that’s gonna be a real issue.

@Tazzmann85 yeah Prates’s KO ability is otherworldly; I’m convinced he can KO anyone in the division. That being said though, I think it’s much harder for him to find the kill shot against younger, more durable guys; especially ones like Jack who keep a tight defensive guard and is pretty durable to begin with. Prates KO power is obviously insane but he also has yet to KO someone under 30 years old or someone who hadn’t been finished before. JDM did get finished in the beginning of his career but I’ve seen pretty much every pro fight he’s had and he has crazy durability/recovery.

I also really think Jack’s style is going to take away from the danger of Prates. If you fight him up close in the pocket, it takes away all of his range/tools. He’s still a good and very dangerous striker but I feel confident in saying his boxing (especially in the pocket) is not close to Jack’s. I don’t want to read into it too much since debuts are always shaky, but Giles boxing & pressure heavy gameplan was causing a ton of issues. I think JDM can enact a similar gameplan; he won’t have to worry about takedowns for once and has the cardio/pace/technical ability to strike with Prates for 25 minutes

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Cam Rowston, DEC
On paper this looks like an easy win for Rowston but I do worry about him here. Bryczek is going to bring the fight to him and Rowston though very promising does have defensive flaws and he did get rocked by Brundage early. The thing is though Bryczek is a bit flawed himself primarily because he is a one dimensional boxer. He can be wild and aggressive and set a pace but if you are a good striker who can manage distance you can make him look bad. Rowston is a promising young prospect and training out of City Kick Boxing I just think he's in there with more dynamic guys than this every day and aslong as he doesnt get chinned I just believe he has way more ways to win this fight.

Tafa vs. Christian
Junior Tafa, TKO, R1
Look man, I know its Junior Tafa and I know he's fighting a grappler but you need to go back and watch this other guy fight on DWCS. Its a miracle he's is in the UFC and I promise he's going to get chinned in the first 3 minutes. Now could Tafa get overaggressive looking to finish and get caught in a sub? Absolutely. But with this matchup they really couldnt have given him any easier guy to KO on the feet so if he engages what so ever in the grappling it would be one of the most low IQ decisions of all time.

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Colby Thicknesse, DEC
This is a sneaky good fight because Thicknesse has not looked particularly good and Morales is a bit of a journeyman on a 3 fight losing streak. These guys have pretty similar strengths and weaknesses so its kind of a coin flip for me and I think the coin flip tie has to guy to the  Australian prospect at home. Morales has a negative decision ratio and I think if Thicknesse is good at anything its keeping it close in certain rounds even though he does no damage at all on the feet. I think he can do just enough here to get the win.

Schultz vs. Johnston
Wes Schultz, DEC
This is a tough one to pick because Wes Schultz is a can but Ben Johnston is this random 35 year old guy they picked up off the street. Ive never watched Johnston fight before but apparently he is a muay thai striking coach and hes got mostly submission wins so that tells me he's probably the more well rounded guy here but he's got so many red flags with his age, inactivity, alleged retirement, and of course the short notice. If I like anything about Schultz its that even though he is a punching bag he does have that dog in him. I could see him getting rocked and socked around a bit on the feet but once he starts shooting for those takedowns and using that college wrestling background I think he's gonna gas this old guy out and grapple his way to a decision or late finish.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef, SUB, R2
This is a tricky one because my initial gut instinct was Micallef is a lock after that Oban Elliot win but then I remembered that he was really making me sweat with that pick cause he was getting taken the school on the feet. Now the thing with this fight is that he's the much more dangerous fighter on the feet and in the BJJ he's very strong as well and I think thats why Im ultimately going to pick him here but watch out for Thembas wrestling here. Hes been training in Russia and we've seen him sort of embrace that wrestling heavy and decision friendly style so I wouldnt be shocked if he goes for that right from the jump. His biggest issue with that is that if Micallef's team is expecting that than I anticipate hes will be looking for guillotines and submisisons off his back which Themba has been caught in before and this is a big strong kid with long arms and long legs.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan, DEC
Im going Don Mar Fan here. Im just not sold on Cody Steele. He had that fun DWCS fight but I think we're starting to learn that the guys who "go to war" on that show dont always pan out to be good in the UFC. Hes a lot mor dangerous on the feet here but I think his striking itself is a bit overrated and I think Mar Fan can turn this into his brand of fight by securing takedowns and winning minutes. Now if Steele defends the takedowns or gets back up every single time then its pretty much his fight to lose, but I really do think this guy Mar Fan has some grit to him and hes going to give him a tough fight. Give me Mar Fan to grind out a close decision.

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Jack Della Maddalena, DEC
Tough fight to call man it’s such a cool one and really good matchmaking FINALLY by the UFC. I’m gonna go with JDM to shut down Prates similar to the Garry fight and have a stellar performance where it’s close but clear that Jack’s won. It’s gonna be such a close contended match for both guys and they both pose a lot of issues for one another. Prates has more power and range but JDM has the speed and skill advantage only if by a bit. He’s got the best boxing in the WW division and can really flow on the feet with someone like Prates with will play into the tit for tat game of who can land cleaner. Of course Jack’s gonna have to deal with the power from Prates but if he can deal with that and not let Carlos land those big shots he can piece him up for 5 or potentially less and get a finish. I’m gonna side with Jack 48-47x2, 49-46 U/D or a TKO in the 3rd or 4th. Prates is so dangerous though it’s gonna be interesting to see how they both deal with eachother because they both have ridiculous sticking ability and KO power. Should be a banger fight but I think JDM’s a bit higher level and if it wasn’t for Islam loving the smell of chest hair so much Jack would still be the champion. LETS GO GIACOMO LETS GO👏👏🇦🇺

Tied for 584th

Prates has the range and power advantage plus the more diverse arsenal of strikes ( Knees,  elbows, spinning stuff.) To go along with his punchs. His flow and finishing instinct when he has the momentum in a fight is insane.

JDM has the better boxing, volume and can steal the rounds far more easily with his activity as Carlos is getting into the fight. He can also use the grappling he has been working so much on to break Prates flow and continue banking rounds.     ( But I don't think he will use any wrestling unless he is serious wobbled and needs a moment to recover).

If it goes to a decision I can only see JDM winning and if it's a Carlos win it will be a TKO/KO.

JDM is not Ian Garry, he is not gonna use footwork and kicks to back away from Prates, he will not shoot non stop to break his flow, he will not engage in long clinchs against the cage. He is gonna try to get close and piece Prates up and I can see him having sucess for a while... and then he is out cold.

A knee down the middle as he advances to close distance with his guard high, a left hand as he expected another knee, a elbow as Carlos holds him in a clinch or hands fight in close range. I just think there is way to many ways for carlos to get him. He will have exactly the fight he wants to have against a striker that is happy to stand and bang.

posts.author: gliese58i
posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 13 Winners, 4 Perfect, 515 Points
|
Tied for 5718th

The main event is so good, I can see this going either way as reflected in the odds.

Maddalena has age, hometown bias, footwork, volume and cardio on his side.
Prates has reach, experience and power.

I think the key for Prates is in honing the outside calf kick in the closed stance because JDM can fight equally well out of either stance whereas Prates is a southpaw that is extremely good at fighting in the open stance. Whenever he fights in open stance he is very good at lead-hand handfighting and can employ kicks knees and punches from his left to the generally open body of his opponents, going low or high.
If JDM can keep it in closed stance southpaw to southpaw as I expect he will try he has much more defensive tools to work with in presenting all of his lead leg and lead arm as an obstacle, on top of his already impressive striking defense.

For Prates I worry obviously about his cardio. I think he fought at a slower pace both in the Garry fight and at the start against Magny (short as that fight was) in a concern to preserve his enery across 25 min. We know he still smokes ciggarettes daily, about 10-15 a day according to one BBC article I found from as recent as march 2025. A habit he developed at age 12 or 13.
Another weakness is his striking defense which is honestly not that good, anyone that tried and succeeding in throwing combinations with multiple shots and pushing Prates back has had a lot of success. He leans out of the way and does not protect with his hands, and he starts from a relatively square Muay Thai stance to begin with.
JDM is a master of those multi-punch flurry attacks mixing between head an body and he should look to overwhelm and push Prates back with those to land something significant and to avoid getting pushed back to the cage where Prates is the most dangerous.

posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.content:
Predictions: 9 of 13 Winners, 4 Perfect, 670 Points
|
Tied for 2190th

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Carlos Prates, TKO, R3
Yes I'm biased, how can I pick against Prates in a striking match. This is an elite striking matchup and one of the best fights you can make right now, two dangerous finishers with very different styles. range vs pocket, did you see that sizemog? Prates is most dangerous at distance where he can use his reach, kicks, and timing to set up knockouts. JDM does his work in the pocket, tight boxing, high volume, and body work. JDM throws in volume and while he hits hard he isn’t gonna KO Prates, he’ll have to be perfect on the front foot for 25 minutes because he can’t be backing up and Carlos doesn’t have bad cardio issues and his power is still there in the fourth and fifth rounds. I think Prates has the right tools to find the shot even if he drops a round or two because of volume, range management with height/reach advantage, elbows, the knee for his high tight guard, calf kicks, teeps. Prates is great at punishing entries and countering if Jack is going forward which could set up something big. Jack gets hit a lot going against guys like Belal and Holland, I can’t see him trying to get into the pocket for 25 minutes against a power puncher like Prates without getting caught. Prates gets his KO and title shot in Perth.

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld, TKO, R1
Oh, Benny…

Elliott vs. Erceg
Steve Erceg, DEC
I can totally see how people think Elliot is a really good dog because of his one of one style and recent performances against Asakura (who can’t grapple), and my guy Su. But stylistically Erceg should have this in the bag. Erceg is probably better everywhere. He has solid boxing, a good frame, and decent grappling with strong scrambles and control ability. He doesn’t always use his BJJ aggressively, but he is hard to hold down and generally manages transitions well. His main issue is defensive striking flaws (especially tall-man defense), but that doesn’t matter here because Tim doesn’t have much power. Tim will likely be able to get Erceg down at times and make it scrappy, but Erceg’s scrambling and ability to get back to his feet should keep him in it. On the feet, Erceg should have the cleaner, more effective striking, Tim’s volume is more awkward than damaging. There is also some risk that Tim’s grappling could create submission openings if he gets careless, especially late if fatigue sets in. 3x 29-28 Erceg.

Rahiki vs. Schmid
Marwan Rahiki, TKO, R2
Rahiki vs. Jenkins would’ve been a banger, but Rahiki survived this time. This should be a lay-up.

Gaziev vs. Peričić
Brando Peričić, TKO, R1
A legit HW prospect. At first look I thought Shamil would win, you hear his name so much you think he’s actually a good fighter but he’s 3-2 in the UFC and the wins are against the worst of the worst, and he had minimal success in his losses, none in the WCA fight. So Brando isn’t even that behind in experience/resume. Gaziev is a slower, blob heavyweight with limited movement and clear cardio issues. He has solid power and decent boxing, but his hand speed is slow, his output drops quickly, and he doesn’t react well under pressure or after getting hit. While he does have some wrestling ability, his top game is mostly heavy but low activity, and he’ll probably gas himself out. Brando is being pushed quickly in the heavyweight division. He’s a big HW with strong athleticism, fast movement for the division, and a K1 striking background, while still being a “kill or be killed” style instead of the overly cautious approach like the rest of CKB. He has decent takedown defense and anti-grappling for a heavyweight, just needs to pace himself. Stylistically, this looks like a major speed and athleticism mismatch, with Brando likely being much faster, sharper, and more dynamic on the feet. He should be able to land first, land cleaner, and consistently beat Gaziev to exchanges.

Tuivasa vs. Sutherland
Tai Tuivasa, DEC
Slop decision alert I would love a shoey but Teixara 2.0. Tai has the better early finishing threat, but this matchup doesn’t scream a quick KO scenario because Louie’s wrestling could allow him to slow the fight down and force clinch and grappling heavy exchanges, even though Louie’s top game isn’t very effective, it may still be enough to stall Tai’s offense and slow the pace of the fight, and neither of these dudes have a gas tank or can fight with the same pace of round one for 15 minutes. It looks less like a quick knockout spot and more like a slow, messy fight where neither guy can fully impose their strengths. Tai likely survives early grappling looks, but doesn’t do enough consistent damage to finish, while Louie’s wrestling slows the pace without leading to meaningful offense. 3x 29-28 Tuivasa.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Robert Bryczek, TKO, R1
I’ve never been on Rowstan. Rowston has improved since his DWCS fight, using his frame better with kicks and a jab, but he still lacks power, struggles badly under pressure, has awful tall man defense, and has shown questionable durability after being hurt and dropped before. His grappling is slow and ineffective most of the time, with weak takedowns. Bryczek is a solid pressure boxer who is at his best walking opponents down, using his fast hands, body work, and pocket work, but he struggles if forced to fight off the back foot. The key is Bryczek’s forward pressure versus Rowston’s range, but Rowston’s inability to handle being forced backward is a major issue, and Bryczek should be able to consistently close distance and win exchanges in the pocket with his fast and powerful combinations. Rowstan's awful defense and sus chin worry me especially the round one with Brundage, Bryczek definitely has the power to end it early. I’m rocking with the crafty polish boxer for the upset.

Tafa vs. Christian
Junior Tafa, TKO, R1
I’m actually confident in a Tafa to win a UFC fight. Tafa is low-IQ but an actual striker with fast hands and power his main issue is his poor grappling and decision making rather than lack of ability on the feet. Kevin is a large but uncoordinated fighter who doesn’t use his reach well, throws looping shots, has low output, limited power, poor defense, and is chinny. He’s a complete bum. Kevin is only in this (odds wise) because of his record and submission wins, but those are misleading since he doesn’t wrestle much, he’s usually the one being taken down which Tafa won’t do. He can really only throw up submissions off his back which isn’t a good reason to pick him. Tafa is a real knockout threat against a punching bag like this.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
Jacob Malkoun, TKO, R1
Oh, GM3…

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Vince Morales, DEC
The line feels wrong. Morales is being undervalued probably because of his record. He’s not a can; he's faced strong competition recently. Morales has the clear striking and pressure advantage, Better BJJ/More dangerous on the mat. He can be taken down but he’s difficult to hold down for long and an active scrambler. Thicknesse style is wrestling and control, and it’s pretty mid with a 9% accuracy and not really any control. His striking is basic and he gets hit a lot. He hasn’t shown much in either the Musasa or Topuria fights, He presents no danger. Morales will outwork him on the feet, and defend enough wrestling. 3x 30-27 Morales.

Schultz vs. Johnston
Wes Schultz, SUB, R1
Regional MMA fight. Very low confidence, I lean Schlutz because I know he can beat a jobber and he has a decent wrestling background/submission game. Johnston is one of the guys that get signed on paramount for no reason at all other than they come from the country of the card. Old and coming off a long layoff, he’s basic and was never special when he did fight. There’s a chance he catches Schultz who can’t strike or take punches but I think Schultz puts on a grappling “masterclass”.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef, SUB, R2
Micallef should win easily because Themba’s wrestling feeds directly into his BJJ, and Themba’s just not that guy. Themba is predictable and is gonna shoot early. That plays directly into Micallef’s best skill set. Micallef will either scramble, reverse, or threaten a submission every time Themba shoots and he'll eventually get one or get enough damage from top to get the fight stopped.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan, DEC
Two mid-level fighters, close matchup, but DMF’s pressure might be the difference. I think Dom Mar Fan’s clinch heavy, cage pushing, chain wrestling style will grind Steele down to a ugly decision win for the local. I liked DMF in the Frogman fight very scrappy. 3x 29-28 Dom Mar Fan.

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Carlos Prates, TKO, R3
I don’t trust the striking defense of Maddalena against a guy with as many weapons as Prates has. I think Prates figures out the striking and catches him exiting early round 3.

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld, TKO, R1
Benny is more skilled than Quillan. However, his chin is completely gone, his coach has talked about him getting KOed in sparring multiple times. He should come out looking to wrestle here, but I think Quillan finds his way back to the feet and catches him with a straight right hand in an exchange late in the first.

Elliott vs. Erceg
Tim Elliott, DEC
People are acting like Erceg’s best win isn’t Matt Schnell. I’m not sure how this fight plays out, but I’m fading Erceg here and going with Tim by 29-28 decision.

Rahiki vs. Schmid
Marwan Rahiki, TKO, R1
Rahiki’s time to get chinned is coming, but I don’t think it’s here against a short notice opponent. Rahiki has a lot of potential and is super fun, I think he gets another highlight here.

Gaziev vs. Peričić
Brando Peričić, TKO, R1
Will be fun as long as it lasts, but Brando is way more athletic than Shamil and Shamil hates being hit, so I think Brando gets it done in about 2 minutes here

Tuivasa vs. Sutherland
Tai Tuivasa, TKO, R1
Sutherland has 10 seconds of cardio for one blitz and a blast double leg. Tuivasa should weather that “storm” and put out a guy in Sutherland who crumbles after being hit.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Cam Rowston, DEC
Great matchmaking here, should be a decent test of what Rowston’s got. I think the leg kicks of Rowston will be a big difference maker against a boxer like Bryczek, and I can’t rate a win over 2025 Brad Tavares at all. Should be a fun competitive fight that could go either way, but I got Cam 29-28.

Tafa vs. Christian
Junior Tafa, TKO, R1
Anyone picking Christian has never seen him fight. This guy does nothing well except throw up subs off his back. Can’t strike and can’t wrestle. Unless Tafa is dumb enough to enter his guard after dropping him, I don’t see a likely way he wins this fight. I can already envision the clubbing overhand Tafa KOs him with.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
Jacob Malkoun, TKO, R1
If Malkoun can’t get a finish here I don’t know what to say. It pains me to see it but GM3 is completely done and missed weight which is a terrible sign.

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Vince Morales, DEC
I don’t rate Thicknesse at all. 2/14 on TDs against a guy who can’t grapple in Musasa? Morales is a competent grappler enough to keep it on the feet, where I think he outstrikes Colby to a 29-28 decision win.

Schultz vs. Johnston
Wes Schultz, SUB, R1
No real reason for this one, but Johnston gives me Julien Leblanc vibes as a 35 year old who came out of a 2 year retirement. At least we know Schultz has good jiu jitsu.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef, SUB, R1
Micallef better in all facets of MMA. I think he hurts Themba on the feet and forces panicked shots and then locks up a front choke or a guillotine.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan, DEC
Fan is better everywhere except on the ground and I don’t think Steele has the wrestling to get it there. DMF 29-28.

posts.content:
Predictions: 9 of 13 Winners, 5 Perfect, 670 Points

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Carlos Prates, TKO, R2
Okay... Im a real big fan of both of these guys so I've bounced back and fourth on who Im gonna pick here since the fight has been announced. Ultimately what broke the tie is my personal believe that Carlos Prates is the most devastating striker in the welterweight division right now. I know Michael Morales might have a thing or two to say about that, but the way Prates is putting these dudes out cold is almost like magic. He can touch you one with a simple straight punch and it'll send you to another dimension. I love watching Jack on the feet and I think he'll be winning most moments of this fight but once that touch of death comes... It's over. And I think particularly in the first two rounds this guy Prates is one of the most dynamic power punchers we've ever seen. Like I really cannot discount how effortlessly he KO's these guys who are reputable UFC fighters. Give me Carlos by KO for the big upset win here.

Rahiki vs. Schmid
Marwan Rahiki, TKO, R2
Look... We all know the UFC is setting up Rahiki for a highlight KO here. The only difference is SCHMIDDY WILL MAKE THIS MASSIVE.

Gaziev vs. Peričić
Brando Peričić, TKO, R1
At first instict I thought about taking Shamil because of the inexperience of Pericic. Only thing is that when I think about it, Shamil isnt exactly a huge test for him. He's going to be the better striker and the better athlete all together so I think that athletism advantage plays a big part in landing first and landing more devastatingly. Still, I have my concerns that Shamil could land a big shot early in the fight, We saw Brando get hurt by Louie Sutherland right off the jump and Shamil has good hands. The thing that has me leaning Brando here though is that Shamil just falls apart the longer the fight goes on. I dont see a world where Shamil gets a win unless its a flash KO right off the bat so even though it breaks my heart, I cant pick him here. Though I will be rooting for him with all my heart.

Tuivasa vs. Sutherland
Tai Tuivasa, TKO, R1
Never thought Id be saying this again but I really like Tai Tuivasa here. We all know about the massive loss streak and how he fell so far from the #3 guy in the world at one point all the way back down to an unranked heavyweight gate keeper. Still, in this one here the UFC is giving him a guy clearly not on his skill level. Louie has had small windows of success literally in the first minute of every UFC fight but I dont think he's ever gotten past the point of landing one big shot. We know Tai is tough and durable and he's certainly been in there with more dangerous guys than Sutherland so I see this one being pretty predictable; Sutherland is going to go right at him and empty the tank and then Tuivasa is going to survive and land the better and cleaner shots to eventually find the round 1 finish. Give me Tai all day, shoey incomg!

Kody Steele vs. Dom Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan via Split Decision
It’s not that I don’t think Kody has potential, it’s just maybe these matchups aren’t going to go his way right now. Street Buddha isn’t going to knock him out but he’ll have the reach and I have to see Kody sharpen up his defense before I believe in it. If he effectively closes the distance and can implement his gameplan in the clinch or even sweep him to the mat, things could get interesting but I’ll take Street Buddha to get the razor thin Split.

Jonathan Micallef vs. Themba Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef via 2nd Round Submission
Now Micallef was someone I can admit I was a bit too low on mainly because I just didn’t see much in his lead up to DWCS. He’s changed that in a hurry because he’s a real Welterweight Prospect that can make some noise. Themba on the other hand is staring at dropping three straight and it’s getting to be nut cuttin’ time for him. Micallef should be able to get his hands on him and likely a RNC by the 2nd but Themba’s not going to roll over for him.

Wes Schultz vs. Ben Johnston
Wes Schultz via 2nd Round Submission
One last time, it’s Party Time down under. I’m not picking a mid-30’s newcomer that hasn’t fought in two years regardless if he’s a hometown favorite. I gotta see it to believe it, Wes locks up a 2nd Round Anaconda to keep his job.

Colby Thicknesse vs. Vince Morales
Vince Morales via Decision
I’m just not sold on Thicknesse and Morales is a tricky case of a bad record but being a tough out. This just gives me that “Yall should know better” vibe and I’ll take the savvy vet to snake the Decision victory.

Jacob Malkoun vs. Gerald Meerschaert III
Jacob Malkoun via 1st Round KO
I love you GM3, but it’s time 🥀.

Junior Tafa vs. Kevin Christian
Junior Tafa via 3rd Round TKO
Another ****** meme fight (We’re having way too many of these lately please stop). They both suck, Christian isn’t UFC caliber and the Tafa Bros are a ****stain on the UFC. I hate this ****, Tafa via 3rd Round Meme KO. **** them both, I think I’ve made my point.

Cam Rowston vs. Robert Bryczek
Cam Rowston via 1st Round TKO
I’m officially a Cam Rowston Believer, come join me as we see our new Aussie hero knock this Polish **** back to Eastern Europe with the quickness.

Tai Tuivasa vs. Louie Sutherland
Tai Tuivasa via 1st Round TKO
****** Meme Fight #2, let’s just be degenerates. Louie is a juice head and isn’t skilled and I think we all know what we need to know about Tai. At least he’ll get a win, and should retire on a high note afterwards.

Shamil Gaziev vs. Brando Pericic
Brando Pericic via 1st Round KO
Hope they reinforced the cage with all these Heavyweights. The Meal is the GOAT but Pericic will put him down with a right hook in the 1st, and we’ll all be sad and asking God why.

Marwan Rahiki vs. Ollie Schmid
Marwan Rahiki via 2nd Round TKO
Freaky Rahiki is getting a favorable matchup with Ollie stepping in but it’ll be a one sided beatdown. Freaky drops him and finishes him with strikes in the 2nd.

Tim Elliott vs. Steve Erceg
Steve Erceg via Decision
Some funky line movement happening before this one. As long as you don’t match Erceg up with a real contender, he should be in his wheelhouse and especially at home in Australia. Elliott can still give anyone some problems but Erceg will pass this test and outclass Elliott for the Decision Victory.

Beneil Dariush vs. Quillan Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld via 1st Round TKO
This feels like a lamb being lead to slaughter for Benny. Also a great showcase fight for Quillan not only being at home but against a former contender that’ll look nice to add to his resume. Benny’s just not what he use to be so I don’t expect this to last very long.

Jack Della Maddalena vs. Carlos Prates
Carlos Prates 2nd Round TKO
This will be the fight to catapult Prates to a title shot. Jack’s style could give him some problems on the feet to start but with Prates reach, creativity and power I just don’t think JDM has a great chance of surviving this. I’ll say Prates catches JDM mid-exchange with a spinning back elbow and stamp himself into title contention.

Dariush vs. Salkilld
I predict Quillan Salkilld at -400. He has a bonkers palm block. Salkilld isn't about to block the cutman.

Elliott vs. Erceg
I trust Steve Erceg at -180. He has a bodacious reverse side kick and a groundbreaking armbar and also a radical takedown. Erceg shall embarass the ring announcer.

Tafa vs. Christian
I go with Junior Tafa at -220. He has a zingy double knife hand block. Tafa should overpower the corner man.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
I trust Jacob Malkoun at -1800. He has a valiant demeanor and an integral mentality and also an incredible stutter step. Malkoun can bully the cycle.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
I finger Cam Rowston at -190. He has a kooky butterfly sweep. Rowston will likely avert the unthinkable.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
I favor Kody Steele at -150. He has a luxurious kneebar and a nerdy anaconda and also a resilient axe kick. Steele must undress the enemy.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
I choose Jonathan Micallef at -225. He has a jinxed crotch-hold. Micallef is gonna ignite the ring announcer.

Thicknesse vs. Morales
I go with Colby Thicknesse at -140. He has a ultimate side kick to the throat and a laudable overhead chop and also a perfect kimura. Thicknesse could wrestle the corner man.

Schultz vs. Johnston

Tuivasa vs. Sutherland

Rahiki vs. Schmid

What a great event. Amazing atmosphere, incredible finishes, and I ended up having a profitable event. However, let's talk about the Malkoun fight. And this is nothing against him personally as I'm sure he's a good guy outside the ring, but inside the ring he has the fight IQ of a PEANUT. He is given a layup fight in his home country to get the crowd fired up to kick off the event. A guy who has terrible striking, a chin with a health bar at 2/100 (Dariush is 1), and not very good wrestling who is pushing 40 years old. All he has to do is bite down on his mouthpiece and throw in the pocket knowing nothing is coming back his way, or throw volume and avoid the takedowns. What does he do? Throw low volume, stay out of the pocket, and attempt takedowns..... Did he think Gerald would turn the clock back, turn into prime Adesanya, and hit him with a huge counter if he wasn't careful? So he shoots a takedown R1, which was really dumb, and lost the round. What does the peanut do R3? Shoot again, TWICE! Bro the main way Gerald wins is if you grapple him... He was so scared of striking with Gerald he wasn't even throwing volume even though Malkoun has great cardio, and I honestly think he could have got him out of there with his light jabs if he just threw it more. It's not even the fact that he didn't get the finish, but that he didn't get anywhere close trying to finish the fight. It must be Gerald's aura, that's the only explanation for what happened there. Seeing the moustache across from you must make you completely throw the gameplan out of the window. He took the absolutely hardest path to victory he could possibly take. Does he not realize what happens when he gets a finish? You get a GUARANTEED 25K, get the crowd fired up, likely time on the mic, bigger fights, and fights higher up on the card. I'm surprised all 3 judges gave him R3 as Gerald has the same significant strikes and tried a sub, but will have to rewatch it. And yes, I did put some bread on him for KO, but it wasn't very much and I still had a profitable night. Moreso just baffled at what he was thinking out there 😂😂😂. It reminded of the Gibson/Blackshear fight where Gibson was touching up Blackshear well on the feet, tries a TD, almost gets submitted R1, and takes Blackshear down a second time for no reason at all. Malkoun got sooooo lucky he got away with that tonight
